GSE268991.reasoning: This series (GSE268991) contains 56 single-cell samples from colorectal cancer patients with paired tumor and adjacent normal colon tissues. All samples are derived from the same anatomical site (colon) and disease state (CRC). The library types indicate three distinct assay modalities: 5'GEX for transcriptomic profiling (scRNA-seq), Surface libraries with ADT antibodies for protein surface detection (CITE-seq), and VDJ libraries for T-cell receptor repertoire sequencing (TCR V(D)J). All samples use 10x Genomics Chromium 5' chemistry as indicated by the library type descriptions. Patient IDs range from CRC 77-91 with consistent sampling across disease states. The high confidence (1.0) reflects unambiguous metadata with clear disease, tissue, and assay specifications across all samples.

GSE269123.reasoning: This series (GSE269123) contains 28 single-cell samples from normal thymus tissue across multiple developmental timepoints (4 days to 5 months postnatal) and both sexes. The metadata clearly indicates all samples are from 'thymus' with source_name 'Vδ1 thymocytes', suggesting a focus on gamma-delta T cell development in the thymus. Samples are split into three distinct library types: ADT (protein surface markers, 8 samples), gene expression/RNA-seq (9 samples), and TCR VDJ sequencing (11 samples - both gdTCR and abTCR). The platform is consistently 10X Genomics based on the description field. No disease condition is specified, so disease_normalized is empty string and tissue_subtype is also empty as these are normal developmental samples rather than tumor or pathological tissue. Confidence is high (0.95) due to clear and consistent metadata across all samples.

GSE287976.reasoning: This series contains 12 samples from healthy human donors (no disease condition specified in metadata). All samples originate from peripheral blood with cell types including PBMCs and sorted basophils (CCR3-enriched). The dataset uses 10X Genomics platform for single-cell profiling. Samples GSM8756665-8756674 represent paired CITE-seq experiments where Gene Expression (mRNA) and Surface Protein (ADT) libraries are sequenced separately but from the same donor/cell type combination, hence classified as CITE-seq assay with corresponding RNA and protein_surface layers. The final two samples (GSM8756675-8756676) use FLT-seq long-read technology from 10X genomics cDNA for transcriptome sequencing only. Tissue subtype is empty as these are healthy control samples without pathological conditions.

GSE294427.reasoning: This series contains 9 GSM entries representing 3 biological samples (numbered 1-3) with separate library files for each molecular layer. All samples are from PBMC with T cell enrichment under ATI treatment condition. The ADT libraries measure surface protein via antibody tags (protein_surface layer), GEX libraries capture polyA RNA gene expression (RNA layer), and TCR libraries capture T-cell receptor V(D)J sequences (TCR_VDJ layer). The 10x Chromium 3' platform is inferred from the CITE-seq format, polyA RNA specification, and HEAL_FILTERED file naming convention typical of 10x Genomics data. No disease state is specified in the metadata, so disease_normalized is left empty. Each GSM represents a distinct library type uploaded separately to GEO, though they originate from the same biological samples.

GSE296447.reasoning: This series contains paired ADT (antibody-derived tag) and GEX (gene expression) libraries from 4 periodontitis patients (P5, P8, P9, P16) sampled from gingival tissue. The explicit 'Citeseq' designation in titles combined with separate ADT and GEX library types indicates a CITE-seq experiment where surface proteins and transcriptomes are measured from the same single cells. The polyA RNA molecule type suggests 3' GEX chemistry, most commonly implemented on 10x Chromium platforms. Disease is inferred from 'Gum_peridontitis' in library names, which normalizes to periodontitis (chronic inflammatory disease of the gums). Tissue 'Gum' is anatomically normalized to 'gingiva'. No tumor or specific subtype information is provided, so tissue_subtype remains empty.

GSE299416.reasoning: This series contains single-cell samples from patients receiving CAR-T cell therapy (Kymriah/tisagenlecleucel), which is approved for B-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia and diffuse large B-cell lymphoma. The disease annotation reflects this hematologic malignancy context since specific cancer type is not explicitly stated in the metadata. All 9 samples are from PBMC with T cell enrichment, collected post-infusion. Three GEX libraries measure gene expression (RNA layer), three VDJ libraries capture TCR repertoire (TCR_VDJ layer), and three ADT libraries quantify surface proteins via antibody tags (protein_surface layer). The platform is consistently 10x Genomics Chromium based on library descriptions. Confidence is set at 0.85 due to the absence of explicit disease diagnosis in metadata, though CAR-T therapy strongly indicates hematologic malignancy.

GSE303197.reasoning: This series (GSE303197) is a single-cell multi-omics study of CD4+ T cells from peripheral blood across multiple donors (OM5334, 5104, 603, GS). The samples include separate library preparations for GEX (RNA), ADT/HTO (protein surface and cell hashing), and TCR V(D)J sequencing. The platform is consistently 10x Chromium 5' based on the read structure (read1: cell barcode + UMI, read2: transcript/VDJ). The disease context is experimental rather than clinical - samples involve ARC clones (likely AAV or lentiviral vectors), dARC (defective ARC), and uninfected controls, suggesting a gene therapy/viral infection model study. All samples are from peripheral blood CD4+ T cells with no tissue subtype differentiation (no tumor/normal distinction). Confidence is high (0.9-0.95) due to clear metadata fields.

GSE306022.reasoning: This series contains 9 samples from 3 experimental conditions (Ci10004, Ci10074, Ci10076), each with paired GEX+FB libraries and TCR sequencing. All samples are CD8 T cells derived from PBMC as explicitly stated in characteristics. The disease status is annotated as 'healthy' since no disease condition is specified and these appear to be control/normal donor cells used for experimental conditions. The GEX+FB samples (6 total) contain polyA RNA, antibody-derived tags (ADT), and hashtag oligonucleotides (HTO), giving them three measured layers: RNA, protein_surface, and cell_label. The TCR samples (3 total) contain V(D)J sequences for immune profiling. Platform is inferred as 10x Chromium 3' based on polyA RNA library type, feature barcoding description, and cell barcode/UMI structure typical of 10x Genomics single-cell technology.

GSE306608.reasoning: This series consists of six samples derived from a CITE-seq experiment on kidney patient-derived xenografts (PDX) with clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC). The samples are split into separate libraries for gene expression (GEX/mRNA), antibody-derived tags (ADT/protein surface), and hashing oligos (HTO/cell label). The platform is inferred as 10x Chromium based on the standard CITE-seq protocol utilizing ADT and HTO. Disease is normalized to clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC) and tissue to kidney, with subtype tumor reflecting the PDX origin.

GSE313153.reasoning: The series contains four samples from two donors (6 and 23) sourced from spleen splenocytes. The presence of paired GEX (polyA RNA) and ADT (protein) libraries for each donor indicates a CITE-seq experimental design, which is typically performed using the 10x Chromium platform with 3' chemistry given the polyA selection. No disease pathology is described, implying healthy control status. Although the RNA and protein libraries are submitted as separate GSMs, they represent a single multi-omics experiment per donor; therefore, all samples are annotated with the CITE-seq assay and corresponding tissue/disease metadata.

GSE313894.reasoning: This series (GSE313894) contains single-cell transcriptomic data from healthy human donors across two tissue types: sigmoid colon (gut-associated immune cells) and PBMC (peripheral blood mononuclear cells). No disease state is explicitly mentioned in the metadata, with samples described as 'Untreated' or stimulated with PMAiIL2, indicating experimental conditions rather than pathological states. The majority of samples (GSM9377203-GSM9377258) are pure scRNA-seq libraries from individual donors, while later samples (GSM9377259-GSM9377264) use cell hashing (HTO) for multiplexing. The final 6 samples (GSM9377265-GSM9377270) are ADT libraries measuring surface protein via antibody tags combined with hash oligos, representing CITE-seq technology. Platform inference as '10x Chromium 3'' is based on polyA RNA selection, single-cell library source, and compatibility with both GEX and ADT measurements. Tissue normalization uses standard anatomical terms (colon for sigmoid colon, PBMC for peripheral blood). Confidence is high (0.95) for samples with clear metadata, slightly lower (0.9) for pooled/hashed samples where donor attribution requires deconvolution.

GSE315668.reasoning: This series (GSE315668) contains 42 samples from PBMC with B cell enrichment across 7 timepoints. The metadata shows three distinct library types: GEX (gene expression, 14 samples), ADT/Feature Barcode (protein surface markers, 14 samples), and BCR (B-cell receptor V(D)J sequencing, 14 samples). All samples are from healthy donors as no disease condition is specified in the metadata. The platform is inferred as 10x Chromium 3' based on '10X GEX data' and 'Feature barcode data' descriptions with polyA RNA molecules. The ADT samples indicate CITE-seq protocol for surface protein measurement, while BCR samples are dedicated immunoglobulin sequencing. Since disease information is absent from all sample characteristics, the series is annotated as healthy. Tissue subtype is empty as these are peripheral blood samples without tumor/normal distinction.

GSE316069.reasoning: This series contains 14 samples from two tissues (colon and PBMC) studying gdT cells. The colon samples (GSM9444084-85) use FLASH-seq technology for scRNA-seq only. The PBMC samples (GSM9444086-97) use 10x Chromium 5' chemistry with separate library preparations for GEX (gene expression), HTO (cell hashing barcodes), and TCR V(D)J repertoire sequencing. All samples are from healthy donors as indicated by 'HD donors' in HTO metadata and absence of disease indicators. The series is dominated by PBMC samples (12/14), so tissue_normalized reflects this majority. Disease is annotated as 'healthy' since no pathological condition is mentioned and explicit reference to healthy donors appears in the metadata.

GSE316096.reasoning: This series (GSE316096) comprises 18 single-cell multi-omics samples from Alzheimer's disease patients treated with lecanemab. All samples are derived from peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs), as explicitly stated in the title and characteristics fields. The experiment uses a 10x Chromium platform, inferred from the combination of transcriptomic single cell library source, ADT antibody tags, and TCR V(D)J sequencing - a characteristic multi-modal single-cell workflow. Each patient has three separate libraries: gene expression (RNA layer), CITE-seq antibody-derived tags (protein_surface layer), and TCR V(D)J repertoire (TCR_VDJ layer). Disease is uniformly Alzheimer's disease across all samples, with APOE genotype variants (E3/E4 or E4/E4) and varying ARIA status (control, ARIA-E, ARIA-H, or combined). Confidence is high (0.95) for all annotations due to explicit metadata fields confirming tissue type, disease state, and assay modalities.

GSE316782.reasoning: This series contains 18 single-cell samples from cancer patients with tumor, lymph node, and normal lung tissues. All samples are explicitly described as 'Multiplexed CITE-seq data' which combines RNA gene expression (GEX) with surface protein detection via antibody tags (ADT), hence measured_layers includes both RNA and protein_surface. The presence of tumor tissue alongside normal lung and lymph node tissues from multiple patients indicates a lung cancer study. CITE-seq experiments are typically performed on 10x Chromium platforms, commonly using the 3' chemistry for gene expression. Tissue_subtype is marked as 'tumor' for samples explicitly labeled as tumor or containing tumor tissue, while lymph node and normal lung samples without explicit tumor designation receive empty subtype strings. Confidence values reflect high certainty for tumor samples (0.9) based on explicit labeling, and slightly lower for lymph nodes (0.85) where metastatic status is not specified.

GSE320155.reasoning: This series contains 60 samples from 10 patients (P061-P080 and P069-P108) studying CD4+ T cells (Treg and Tconv subsets) in liver cancer contexts. The first 5 patients have liver metastasis (likely colorectal cancer with liver mets), while the remaining 5 patients have primary hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC). Each patient has 6 separate GSMs: Treg GEX, Treg ADT, Treg VDJ, Tconv GEX, Tconv ADT, and Tconv VDJ. The platform is inferred as 10x Chromium based on polyA RNA selection, ADT protein surface markers, and TCR V(D)J enrichment which are characteristic of this technology. Measured layers are separated by GSM: RNA for GEX/mRNA libraries, protein_surface for ADT libraries, and TCR_VDJ for TCR libraries. The assay is scRNA-seq for GEX samples, CITE-seq for ADT-containing experiments, and TCR V(D)J for receptor sequencing. Confidence is high (0.9) due to clear evidence from titles and characteristics fields.
